action.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

2 gloves, 3 tomatoes, 1 pan, 1 battery

Your secret value function is:

glove: 2, tomato: 0, pan: 3, battery: 3

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content: I value the pan and battery the most, while I don't care about the tomatoes. I'm willing to take the gloves if needed, but my priority is securing the pan and battery. What are your priorities?
action.label: response

action.content: I value the pan and battery the most, while I don't care about the tomatoes. I'm willing to take the gloves if needed, but my priority is securing the pan and battery. What are your priorities?

You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.

(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

2 gloves, 3 tomatoes, 1 pan, 1 battery

Your secret value function is:

glove: 0, tomato: 1, pan: 6, battery: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.


I value the pan and battery the most, while I don't care about the tomatoes. I'm willing to take the gloves if needed, but my priority is securing the pan and battery. What are your priorities?
action.label: context

action.content: I value the tomatoes highly, while the pan and battery are my top priorities as well. Since we both want the pan and battery, we need to find a way to split those or trade other items to make it work. I have 3 tomatoes and 2 gloves available to offer. Would you be willing to give up the pan or battery in exchange for all the tomatoes and gloves? Or perhaps we can split the high-value items if you value the tomatoes more than I do? Let me know your thoughts on how to divide the pan and battery.
action.label: response

action.content: I value the tomatoes highly, while the pan and battery are my top priorities as well. I value the tomatoes highly, while the pan and battery are my top priorities as well. Since we both want the pan and battery, we need to find a way to split those or trade other items to make it work. I have 3 tomatoes and 2 gloves available to offer. Let me know your thoughts on how to divide the pan and battery.
action.label: context

action.content:
That sounds like a good starting point. Since I value the tomatoes at 0, I have no use for them, so I am happy to give you all 3 tomatoes and the 2 gloves.

However, I need to clarify my position on the pan and battery. Both are worth 3 points to me, which is my maximum value. I cannot give up either one without receiving something of equal value in return, and since I value the tomatoes and gloves at 0, a trade of those items for a pan or battery doesn't work for me.

If you also value the pan and battery highly, we have a conflict. We cannot both get them. To maximize our scores, one of us must get both the pan and the battery, while the other gets the tomatoes and gloves. Since I get 0 points from tomatoes and gloves, I would get 6 points if I get the pan and battery. If I give them to you, I get 0 points.

Unless you value the tomatoes or gloves significantly more than the pan and battery (which seems unlikely given your message), the only way for me to get a positive score is to keep the pan and battery. Are you willing to take the 3 tomatoes and 2 gloves in exchange for me keeping the pan and battery? This would give you your tomatoes and gloves, and me my high-value items.

[Proposal: 1 pan, 1 battery]
action.label: response

action.content: The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.
action.label: context
